objective: Deliver a runnable SQLite-backed journal, outbox and inbox with idempotent append, delivery lease, ACK/NACK, crash recovery, consumer resume and ordered replay.
non_goals: Do not implement a WebSocket or control-plane server; transport belongs to M01-S4.
Do not implement service supervision, resource scheduling, model inference, speech, memory, avatar, game or livestream behavior.
Do not run repeat/soak/full workflow verification in fast-development mode.
contracts: Only EventEnvelope v1 values that pass the existing Python boundary validator and contain streamId, sequence and idempotencyKey may enter durable delivery.
Journal append and inbox receive are idempotent for byte-identical canonical envelopes and fail closed on conflicting reuse.
Outbox delivery uses bounded claims with expiring leases; ACK is idempotent and NACK stores only a stable redacted error code.
Inbox checkpoints advance only across contiguous processed sequences, so resume cannot skip a gap.
Replay is ordered by stream sequence and all database state survives process close/reopen.
allowed_dependencies: Python 3.12+ standard library sqlite3 and the existing local hina-contracts package.
acceptance_criteria: Appending the same event twice produces one journal/outbox row; conflicting event or stream-sequence reuse fails with a stable code.
An unacknowledged in-flight event is reclaimable after lease expiry with incremented attempt count while acknowledged events are not redelivered.
Duplicate inbox delivery is ignored and consumer checkpoint cannot advance over an unprocessed sequence gap.
Closing and reopening the SQLite database preserves journal, ACK state, inbox state and checkpoint.
The owner can run one PowerShell demo and see crash recovery, ACK, resume and replay results plus a redacted error log.
Focused unit tests for M01-S2 and M01-S3 complete once on the owner machine.
